title: dactylorhiza elata var. algerica
intro: Dactylorhiza elata var. algerica, also known as the Algerian Marsh Orchid, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Dactylorhiza elata orchid species. This terrestrial orchid is native to the mountainous regions of Algeria, and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal remedies. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, offering potential health benefits such as reduced blood pressure and improved cardiovascular health. Dactylorhiza elata var. algerica is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its availability and use may be limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
